name: Perfume / Cologne
description: Perfume, cologne, aftershave, and other fragrance sprays. These are considered liquids under TSA and IATA rules and must comply with the 3-1-1 liquids rule in carry-on baggage.
tsaGuideline: Perfume in carry-on must follow the 3-1-1 rule: containers must be 3.4 oz (100 ml) or less and must fit in one quart-sized clear plastic bag. Larger bottles of perfume must be packed in checked luggage. Perfume is also considered a flammable liquid, so quantities in checked luggage are limited.
iataGuideline: Perfume is classified as a flammable liquid (Class 3 dangerous good) in large quantities. In passenger aircraft, up to 2 kg or 2 liters total of toiletries including perfume are permitted in checked baggage per passenger, with individual containers not exceeding 0.5 kg or 500 ml.
capacityLimit: Carry-on: 100 ml per container. Checked: up to 2 liters total toiletries; individual containers max 500 ml
restrictions: Carry-on: containers must be 100 ml (3.4 oz) or less
Carry-on: all liquids must fit in one quart/liter-sized clear bag
Checked: max 500 ml per individual container
Checked: total limit of 2 liters for all toiletries combined (per IATA)
Extremely large quantities may be classified as dangerous goods

faqs.question: Can I bring a full-size perfume bottle on a plane?
faqs.answer: Full-size perfume bottles (typically 50–100+ ml) can be brought in your carry-on only if each container is 100 ml or less. Bottles larger than 100 ml must go in checked luggage. If you have a 200 ml bottle, it must be checked.

faqs.question: How many perfume bottles can I bring in checked luggage?
faqs.answer: Under IATA rules, you can bring up to 2 liters total of toiletries (including perfume, hairspray, etc.) in checked luggage, with no single container exceeding 500 ml. Individual airlines may have slightly different rules.

faqs.question: Can I bring perfume as a gift on a plane?
faqs.answer: Yes, as long as it follows the rules. In carry-on, perfume must be in 100 ml or smaller containers. In checked luggage, standard retail-sized perfume bottles are fine. Gift-wrapped items may need to be unwrapped at security.
